cityDNA.coreFantasy: A frogfolk marsh city where canals, lock houses, nursery pools, public relief, healing halls, grain markets, and reed-bundle government make wetland life civic rather than rustic.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Pollyr Fen is not a swamp village; the uploaded image pass shows a dense amphibious city of platforms, canals, boats, granaries, nursery basins, healing courts, and civic service halls.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Pollyr Fen turns marsh water, frogfolk life cycles, public care, grain logistics, and flood control into a complete civic system.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Reed roofs, wet stone platforms, canal bridges, boats, lily pads, blue-green beacon light, lock houses, nursery basins, relief plazas, apothecary courts, grain halls, awnings, and misty marsh towers.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Frogfolk civic culture built around water routing, spawning care, communal relief, healing, food storage, and choral public gathering.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Reed bundles, frog-dam choirs, lock wheels, canal reflections, nursery shells, healing lanterns, awnings, grain halls, and waterwheel courts symbolize survival through shared marsh stewardship.

canonLocks: Pollyr Fen is a mature frogfolk marsh city, not a primitive swamp settlement.
The Wonder is Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh.
The Beacon is Lock House Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court.
The five primary districts are Canal / Riverworks, Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Civic Services, Medical / Healing, and Granary / Food Market.

The uploaded district anchors include Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, and Mill Court Nook.
provisionalCanon: Pollyr Fen's highest civic virtue is mutual adaptation.
The contact-sheet pass reinforces the city as a wet, platformed, boat-connected civic marsh rather than wilderness with buildings.
Nursery, relief, healing, and grain systems should remain emotionally central, not support scenery.
rejectedReads: nursery and relief systems as background flavor
cautionFlags: Keep amphibious movement visible through docks, canals, basins, platforms, and wet thresholds.
Do not let the Frog-Dam Choir erase the civic waterworks, healing, food, and relief systems.

environment.environmentalLogic: Water is road, risk, food source, nursery, ritual field, and public utility. Pollyr Fen builds with the marsh rather than against it.

forms: Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court
Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh
skyline: Frog-Dam Choir wonder rising from marsh water
Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court as central authority
Lock House Beacon as waterworks signal
misty lock towers, granary roofs, healing halls, nursery domes, and reed platforms

civilization.people: Frogfolk citizens move easily between water and platforms, with public life centered on canal timing, nursery care, food supply, healing, relief, and communal song.
civilization.governance: The Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court governs through water rights, nursery protection, relief eligibility, healing duty, and grain provisioning.
civilization.religion: Religious practice is marsh-choral and life-cycle centered, carried through frog-dam singing, spawning protection, reed-bundle vows, healing lights, and water reflection rites.

streets.appearance: Wet, crowded, layered, and amphibious: boardwalks, canals, reed roofs, lily pads, boats, lock towers, blue lamps, healing stalls, nursery basins, grain awnings, and relief counters fill the image set.
streets.movement: Movement follows boats, bridges, boardwalks, lock paths, nursery edges, healing courts, relief plazas, and grain-market platforms.
streets.lighting: Mist and wet stone soften daylight while blue-green civic lamps and warm market or healing lanterns guide night movement.
streets.weatherEffects: Rain, floodwater, fog, and high marsh levels change access routes and make lock systems essential.
streets.dayCycle: Waterworks checks, nursery care, healing errands, relief notices, and grain markets dominate daytime.
streets.nightCycle: Night is blue lamps on water, guarded nursery pools, quieter canals, and Frog-Dam song carrying through mist.

housing.wealthy: Raised registry and waterworks homes with dry upper rooms, carved reed screens, shell windows, and private docks.
housing.middle: Platform houses near markets, locks, or healing courts with boat hooks, water steps, and shared roofed porches.
housing.poor: Low wet platforms, reed-and-wood dwellings, shared docks, waterline storage, and close dependence on relief halls and grain markets.

districts.name: Canal / Riverworks
districts.civicPurpose: Flood control, lock timing, canal routing, public signal response, and basic movement.
districts.visualIdentity: Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, wet steps, boats, and blue-green signal light.

districts.name: Nursery / Spawning Grounds
districts.civicPurpose: Protected frogfolk life-cycle care, family continuity, and guarded communal vulnerability.
districts.visualIdentity: Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, low water courts, caregivers, and soft lamps.

districts.name: Civic Services
districts.civicPurpose: Public aid, notices, relief filing, and communal support during water or food stress.
districts.visualIdentity: Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, queues, papers, lanterns, and wet civic paving.

districts.name: Medical / Healing
districts.civicPurpose: Healing, apothecary care, recovery, and marsh medicine.
districts.visualIdentity: Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, herb tables, boats, and warm lamps.

districts.name: Granary / Food Market
districts.civicPurpose: Food security, grain storage, mill work, markets, and marshland provisioning.
districts.visualIdentity: Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, sacks, boats, awnings, and market crowds.

wonder.name: Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh
wonder.symbolicRole: Marsh, voice, dam, community, and frogfolk life made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Wonder-scale frog-dam choir structure rising from water, reed platforms, and misty marsh civic routes.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide gathering site for marsh songs, seasonal water rites, flood memory, and communal reassurance.
storyHooks: A choir pattern warns the marsh before officials do.

beacon.name: Lock House Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Water control and public warning made one signal.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-green beacon rising from lock-house waterworks and canal crossings.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates flood response, boat routes, nursery protection, relief halls, healing, and grain movement.
storyHooks: A Beacon change forces every boat, nursery, and granary route to recalculate.

seatOfPower.name: Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Wetland law bundled from water, family, food, and care obligations.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central court with reed-bundle patterns, wet terraces, civic stairs, registry counters, and marsh-facing authority.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs water rights, nursery protections, relief status, grain measures, healing obligations, and public petitions.
storyHooks: A reed-bundle entry decides whether a family is owed dry ground or open water.

majorLandmarks.name: Nursery Basin
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Shell-inlaid water basin with spawning pool nook, caregivers, low walls, and soft lamps.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Life-cycle care, nursery protection, and family continuity.
storyHooks: A nursery water change makes a canal decision morally urgent.

majorLandmarks.name: Relief Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Marsh care organized as public duty.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Service hall with lantern monument, notice plaza, queues, and wet paving.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Relief, notices, public aid, and response coordination.
storyHooks: A notice proves the marsh counted someone before the registry did.

majorLandmarks.name: Healing Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Healing hall with apothecary court, lantern monument, herbs, and water access.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Medical care, apothecary work, triage, and recovery.
storyHooks: A marsh remedy explains what official medicine missed.

majorLandmarks.name: Grain Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Granary and food market with awning monument, mill court, sacks, boats, and market tables.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Grain storage, market distribution, milling, and food accountability.
storyHooks: A mill tally reveals why the city fears a dry season more than a flood.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House Beacon, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, boats, lock workers, wet platforms, and canal water.
S2 must show Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, caregivers, protected pools, and soft lamps.
S3 must show Civic Services Hall, Relief Hall, Civic Services Lantern Monument, Notice Plaza Nook, queues, notices, and wet civic paving.
S4 must show Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, herb stalls, and canal access.
S5 must show Granary / Food Market Civic Hall, Grain Hall, Awning Monument, Mill Court Nook, sacks, boats, awnings, and market crowds.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh, Lock House Beacon, and Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court legible together.
Use a dense amphibious marsh city with canals, boats, lily pads, wet stone, reed roofs, public care halls, nursery basins, healing courts, and grain markets.
requiredVisualElements: Polliweir Frog-Dam Choir of the Living Marsh
Reed Bundle Patterns Sovereign Registry Court
elementsToAvoid: dry streets with no amphibious movement
